content:
type: REVISE
my_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
your_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: We both touch InternationalizationExtension.parse() (the same while-loop over trans-tag params) and _make_node() in ext.py, so a clean file split isn't possible. Proposal to keep our diffs mergeable: (1) In parse()'s param loop, each of us adds ONE new elif branch recognizing our own keyword(s) (mine: note/created/modified with required string-literal assignment; yours: domain presumably as bare name or name=value) - place elifs as separate, self-contained blocks so they don't rewrite each other's lines. (2) In _make_node(), each of us appends ONE new parameter at the end of the signature/call (mine: metadata: t.Dict[str,str] = {}) and adds our own logic as a distinct new block near the end of the function (mine: 'if metadata: output_node.trans_metadata = metadata' right before return), not touching the same lines you touch. Can you share exactly which lines/keyword('domain') and what you add to _make_node's signature/call so I place my metadata param after yours and avoid line collisions?

content:
type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
your_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: Accepted. Since we appear to be in isolated repo copies anyway, I'll proceed now: I'll only touch keyword detection for note/created/modified (string-literal only, added to a separate metadata dict, not the variables dict) in the parse() loop, and add a new metadata param to _make_node() plus 'if metadata: output_node.trans_metadata = metadata' near the end before return - won't touch domain/dgettext logic. Will send DONE when finished.

content:
type: DONE
my_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: Metadata (note/created/modified) implementation complete: parse() detects the 3 keywords with required string-literal assignment (TemplateAssertionError otherwise), stores them in a separate metadata dict (kept out of variables so rendering is unaffected), and _make_node() now takes a metadata param and sets output_node.trans_metadata only when non-empty. All 830 existing tests still pass; verified all example syntaxes from the spec including combos with variables/trimmed/pluralize and babel_extract. Didn't touch domain/gettext-selection logic.
